response:
VERDICT: TAMPERED
ROUND: 20
REASONING: In round 20 the Majority value is wrong.  
• Inputs for the round are shown in L0349.  
• Converting those 32-bit words to hex we obtain  
 a = 0x60CB6275, b = 0x1C08EC93, c = 0x9509A1FB.  
• The correct majority is (a & b) ⊕ (a & c) ⊕ (b & c) = 0x1409E0F3.  
• The trace (L0357) gives maj = 0001 1100 0000 1000 1110 1100 1101 1011 = 0x1C08ECDB, which is different (only the upper nibble matches).  
• Because S0 (L0356) is later added to this wrong maj to form temp2 (L0358) and every subsequent value uses that result, this is the single-bit corruption that propagates consistently afterwards. Everything before round 20 is consistent, everything after round 20 is self-consistent with the tampered maj, so the single alteration is in round 20.
